title: Is Berlin Expensive? - Quick Summary
items: Berlin sits in the **middle of Europe's price map** — cheaper than Paris, London, or Amsterdam, but more expensive than Prague or Budapest. Most travelers spend **€120–250 per day**.
**Accommodation** is the biggest line item. Hostel dorm €30–45, 3-star hotel €90–130, 4-star €160–250 per night. Off-center neighborhoods (Wedding, Neukölln, Moabit) save 30–50%.
**Food stays affordable** if you avoid tourist strips. Bakery breakfast €3–6, döner €6–8, mid-range dinner €18–28. Lunch menus (Mittagstisch) are dramatically cheaper than dinner.
**Public transport** is one of Berlin's best deals: day ticket **€11.20**, 7-day ticket **€44** (€6.30/day). FEX from BER airport is **€5** — vs. €50–65 by taxi.
**Many of the best things are free**: Reichstag dome (book ahead), Brandenburg Gate, Holocaust Memorial, East Side Gallery, Mauerpark. A €0 attraction budget still hits the essentials.
